summary: After every sensitive cache/store/config/profile/feedback/export/deliver write, added an explicit os.Chmod (0o600 files / 0o700 dirs) so files/dirs left by an older 0o644/0o755 build get re-secured on next write.
reason: os.WriteFile(path, data, 0o600) and os.OpenFile(..., O_CREATE, 0o600) do not change mode bits on an existing file (O_CREATE is a no-op then), and os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o700) does not change an existing dir's mode. Sensitive files created by an older world-readable build stayed 0o644 forever.
validated_outcome: go build/vet/test and verify_skill all pass. profile.go and deliver.go files were already safe via a tmp+rename (0o600 tmp) pattern; only their parent dirs needed re-securing. store.go already chmod'd the SQLite file/WAL/SHM sidecars; only its db dir needed re-securing.
